heading: Vessels stricken from Naval Vessel Register: transfer by gift or otherwise for use as artificial reefs
label: 8676b Vessels stricken from Naval Vessel Register: transfer by gift or otherwise for use as artificial reefs

children.heading: .—Authority To Make Transfer
children.label: a .—Authority To Make Transfer
children.text: The Secretary of the Navy may transfer, by gift or otherwise, any vessel stricken from the Naval Vessel Register to any State, Commonwealth, or possession of the United States, or any municipal corporation or political subdivision thereof, for use as provided in subsection (b).

children.heading: .—Vessel To Be Used as Artificial Reef
children.label: b .—Vessel To Be Used as Artificial Reef

children.text: the recipient use, site, construct, monitor, and manage the vessel only as an artificial reef in accordance with the requirements of the National Fishing Enhancement Act of 1984 ( et seq.), except that the recipient may use the artificial reef to enhance diving opportunities if that use does not have an adverse effect on fishery resources (as that term is defined in section 3(14) of the Magnuson-Stevens Fishery Conservation and Management Act ()); and33 U.S.C. 210116 U.S.C. 1802(14)

the recipient obtain, and bear all responsibility for complying with, applicable Federal, State, interstate, and local permits for using, siting, constructing, monitoring, and managing the vessel as an artificial reef.

children.heading: .—Preparation of Vessel for Use as Artificial Reef
children.label: c .—Preparation of Vessel for Use as Artificial Reef

children.text: the environmental best management practices developed pursuant to section 3504(b) of the Bob Stump National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2003 (; note); andPublic Law 107–31416 U.S.C. 1220

children.heading: .—Cost Sharing
children.label: d .—Cost Sharing
children.text: The Secretary may share with the recipient of a vessel transferred under subsection (a) any costs associated with transferring the vessel under that subsection, including costs of the preparation of the vessel under subsection (c).

children.heading: .—No Limitation on Number of Vessels Transferable to Particular Recipient
children.label: e .—No Limitation on Number of Vessels Transferable to Particular Recipient
children.text: A State, Commonwealth, or possession of the United States, or any municipal corporation or political subdivision thereof, may be the recipient of more than one vessel transferred under subsection (a).

children.heading: .—Additional Terms and Conditions
children.label: f .—Additional Terms and Conditions
children.text: The Secretary may require such additional terms and conditions in connection with a transfer authorized by subsection (a) as the Secretary considers appropriate.

children.heading: .—Construction
children.label: g .—Construction
children.text: Nothing in this section shall be construed to establish a preference for the use as artificial reefs of vessels stricken from the Naval Vessel Register in lieu of other authorized uses of such vessels, including the domestic scrapping of such vessels, or other disposals of such vessels, under this chapter or other applicable authority.
